title: Man page generation research
content:
## How cmdliner generates man pages

Cmdliner generates man pages at **runtime**, not build time. Every cmdliner-based tool automatically supports `--help=groff`, which outputs the tool's help in groff man page format. The man page content is derived from:

- `Cmd.info` (name, synopsis, description)
- Argument `doc` strings (positional and optional)
- `Cmd.Exit.info` and `Cmd.Env.info` entries
- Manual `man` blocks passed to `Cmd.info`

The `Cmdliner.Manpage.print` function can also be used programmatically with format `Groff`.

## How other tools ship man pages

Dune, cmdliner itself, and cmarkit all install man pages into `~/.opam/default/man/man1/`. Cmdliner 2.x ships a dedicated `cmdliner install tool-manpages` command that runs the tool executable, extracts groff output for each subcommand, and writes man pages to a target directory. It can also patch a `.install` file for opam integration.

## Effort to enable man page installation

For dune >= 3.5 with directory-targets, it is straightforward. Add to bin/dune:

```
(rule
 (target (dir cmdliner-support))
 (deps main.exe)
 (action
  (ignore-stdout
   (run cmdliner install tool-support ./main.exe:bs cmdliner-support))))

(install
 (section share_root)
 (dirs (cmdliner-support/share as .)))
```

This generates man pages for bs and all subcommands during `dune build @install`. Alternatively, the simpler opam-only approach uses an opam.template file with a `cmdliner install tool-support --update-opam-install` post-build step.

## Recommendation

For an initial release, `--help` (and `--help=groff` for power users) is sufficient. Man page installation adds a small amount of build complexity and a hard dependency on cmdliner's install tool being available. It is a nice-to-have for a polished release but not blocking.

title: Plan: feature gaps for agent-oriented knowledge bases
content:
## Required reading

- `docs/index.md`, `docs/lib/architecture.md`, `docs/lib/correct-construction.md`
- `docs/lib/principles.md`, `docs/bin/principles.md`, `docs/test/principles.md`
- `docs/test-integration/architecture.md`, `docs/test-integration/principles.md`
- `docs/product-requirements.md`
- `docs/designs/design-feature-gaps.md` — the design being implemented

## Goal

Implement 9 features from the feature gaps design: timestamps, repo delete ops, reopen, delete, unrelate, graph traversal, list query expansion, statistics, and GC. These close gaps that limit `bs` as a primary tool for coding agents.

## Current state

**Data types** — `Todo.t`/`Note.t`: `id`, `niceid`, `title`, `content`, `status`. No timestamps. `make` takes 5 positional args.

**Repository** — `Relation` has no per-relation `delete` or `delete_by_entity`. `Niceid` has no per-entry `delete`. JSONL `entity_record` has no timestamp fields.

**Service** — No `reopen`, `delete`, `unrelate`, `Graph_service`, or `Gc_service`. `Query_service.list` takes individual params, no sort/count/relation-filter support.

**CLI** — 13 commands. No reopen/delete/unrelate/gc. `list` has `--status`, `--available`, `--json` only. `show` has no timestamp lines.

## Target state

**Data types** — `Todo.t`/`Note.t` gain `created_at:int`, `updated_at:int`. New `Data.Timestamp` module. `make` gains `~created_at ~updated_at`.

**Repository** — `Relation` gains `delete`, `delete_by_entity`. `Niceid` gains `delete`. JSONL `entity_record` gains timestamps. Schema adds timestamp columns.

**Service** — `Mutation_service` gains `reopen`, sets `updated_at`. New `Delete_service` (cascade + blocking guard). `Relation_service` gains `unrelate`. New `Graph_service` (BFS). `Query_service.list` takes `list_spec` record. New `Gc_service`. GC hooks into `open_kb`.

**CLI** — 4 new commands: reopen, delete, unrelate, gc. `list` gains `--sort`, `--asc`, `--count`, relation filters, `--transitive`. `show` gains timestamp lines. 17 commands total.

## Design decisions

1. **Timestamps in data types, not a wrapper.** More invasive but cleaner — types fully describe the entity, no indirection.
2. **`updated_at` is a service concern.** `with_*` updaters don't touch timestamps. Service sets it after no-op detection. Keeps data layer pure.
3. **Application-level BFS.** Matches `find_blockers` pattern. Testable, sufficient for scale. API is implementation-agnostic.
4. **Query spec record.** Replaces growing param list with `list_spec` record. Idiomatic, self-contained validation.
5. **Service-layer delete cascade.** Not SQL cascade. Matches thin-repo + service-logic pattern.
6. **GC hooks into `open_kb`.** Every command sees clean state. One change site. Consistent with `rebuild_if_needed`.

## Atomic groups

**Group A: Timestamps** (tasks 1.1–1.4) — Changing `make` signatures breaks compilation everywhere. Data type, repo, JSONL, and service changes are inseparable.

**Group B: Delete cascade** (tasks 4.1–4.3) — Service requires repo ops from step 2. CLI is the only consumer. Land together.

**Group C: List query expansion** (tasks 7.1–7.3) — Changing `list` from params to record breaks the Kb_service → Query_service → Cmd_list chain.

title: Plan: uninstall command and init action refactor
content:
## Required reading

- `docs/lib/architecture.md` — layering rules, where new code belongs
- `docs/lib/principles.md` — correct construction, file length, nesting
- `docs/bin/principles.md` — CLI conventions, `--json` requirement, help text, integration test mandate
- `docs/test/principles.md` — state assertions, temp file cleanup, file length
- `docs/test-integration/principles.md` — cleanup, file length
- `docs/test-integration/architecture.md` — naming, helpers, coverage expectations

## Goal

Implement a `bs uninstall` command that cleanly reverses the effects of `bs init`. The init side effects (database creation, config persistence, AGENTS.md installation, git exclude entry) are currently entangled in `Lifecycle.init_kb` with no reverse path. The change refactors init's side effects into discrete helpers and adds reverse counterparts, then builds a new `uninstall` CLI command on top. Init's observable behavior must not change.

## Current state

`Lifecycle.init_kb` performs five sequential steps inline:

```ocaml
(* lifecycle.ml — current structure *)
let init_kb ~directory ~namespace ~gc_max_age =
  let* directory = resolve_directory directory in
  let* namespace = resolve_namespace ~directory namespace in
  let db_file = Filename.concat directory db_filename in
  if Sys.file_exists db_file then Error (Validation_error ...)
  else
    (* Database creation — inline *)
    let root = Root.init ~db_file ~namespace in
    Fun.protect ~finally:(fun () -> Root.close root) (fun () ->
      Config.set ... ;  Config.set ... ;
      let agents_md = install_agents_md ~directory in
      let git_exclude = install_git_exclude ~directory in
      Ok { directory; namespace; db_file; agents_md; git_exclude })
```

Helpers `install_agents_md` and `install_git_exclude` exist as standalone functions. Database creation is inline. There are no reverse helpers and no `uninstall_kb` function. `Kb_service` re-exports init types verbatim.

Types:
```ocaml
type agents_md_action = Created | Appended | Already_present
type git_exclude_action = Excluded | Already_excluded
type init_result = { directory; namespace; db_file; agents_md; git_exclude }
```

No `delete_file` in `Control.Io`. `Sys.remove` used only in test helpers.

## Target state

`lifecycle.ml` gains three uninstall helpers alongside the existing install helpers, plus an `uninstall_kb` orchestrator:

```ocaml
(* New types *)
type file_action = Deleted | Not_found
type agents_md_uninstall_action =
  | File_deleted | Section_removed | Section_modified | Not_found
type git_exclude_uninstall_action = Entry_removed | Entry_not_found

type uninstall_result = {
  directory   : string;
  database    : file_action;
  jsonl       : file_action;
  agents_md   : agents_md_uninstall_action;
  git_exclude : git_exclude_uninstall_action;
}

(* New helpers *)
val uninstall_file        : string -> file_action
val uninstall_agents_md   : directory:string -> agents_md_uninstall_action
val uninstall_git_exclude : directory:string -> git_exclude_uninstall_action
val uninstall_kb          : directory:string option -> (uninstall_result, error) result
```

Database creation is extracted to `install_database` (internal helper, not exposed). `init_kb` calls it instead of inline code — same behavior.

`Kb_service` re-exports the new types and `uninstall_kb`.

`bin/cmd_uninstall.ml` is a new CLI command gated by `--yes`, supporting `-d` and `--json`. Registered in `main.ml`.

## Impact analysis

**lib/service/lifecycle.ml** — Extract `install_database`, add 3 uninstall helpers + `uninstall_kb` orchestrator + new types. ~80 lines added.
**lib/service/lifecycle.mli** — Export new types and `uninstall_kb`. ~20 lines added.
**lib/service/kb_service.ml** — Re-export uninstall types, add `uninstall_kb` wrapper. ~15 lines added.
**lib/service/kb_service.mli** — Re-export uninstall types and signature. ~15 lines added.
**bin/cmd_uninstall.ml** — New file. CLI argument parsing, `--yes` gate, output formatting (text + JSON). ~80 lines.
**bin/main.ml** — Add `Cmd_uninstall.cmd` to subcommand group + help text. ~3 lines changed.
**test/lifecycle_expect.ml** — New unit tests for each uninstall helper and roundtrip. ~100 lines added.
**test-integration/uninstall_expect.ml** — New file. Integration tests for all 8 scenarios. ~200 lines.

**Files that do NOT change** (worth confirming):
- `lib/service/git.ml` — uninstall reads `.git/info/exclude` directly via `Io.read_file`, no new Git module functions needed.
- `lib/control/io.ml` — uses `Sys.remove` directly for deletion, no new Io helper.
- `bin/cmd_init.ml` — init behavior unchanged.
- `test/lifecycle_expect.ml` existing tests — must pass without modification.
- `test-integration/init_expect.ml` — must pass without modification.

## Design decisions

1. **`Sys.remove` directly, no `Io.delete_file` wrapper.** The design doc notes that no file deletion exists in production code. `uninstall_file` uses `Sys.remove` directly rather than adding to `Io`. Deletion is a one-off need specific to uninstall, not a general utility. If more callers emerge, extraction is trivial.

2. **`install_database` is internal.** Extracted from `init_kb` for clarity but not exposed in `.mli`. It is not independently testable via the public interface — the existing `init_kb` tests cover it. This avoids expanding the module's public API for a refactoring convenience.

3. **`--yes` checked in CLI layer.** Following the `--force` pattern in `cmd_delete.ml`, the confirmation gate is in `cmd_uninstall.ml`. The service function `uninstall_kb` always proceeds. This keeps the safety check where it belongs (user-facing layer).

4. **Uninstall uses `resolve_directory` directly, not the full init precondition chain.** It does not validate namespace or check database existence — partial teardown is supported by the best-effort property.

5. **`Section_modified` warns without removing.** When the AGENTS.md heading is present but the exact template does not match, uninstall reports the discrepancy rather than attempting heuristic removal.

6. **`String.contains_substring_at` not needed.** The design uses `String.sub` for index-based extraction in `uninstall_agents_md`. This is implemented directly using stdlib `String` functions rather than adding a new `Data.String` helper.

## Atomic groups

**Group A: Types + helpers + orchestration** (tasks 2–5)
Tasks 2–5 add new types and functions to `lifecycle.ml`/`.mli`. Each task adds types needed by subsequent tasks. They can be committed individually (each leaves the codebase compiling), but they form a logical unit — the uninstall service layer.

**Group B: CLI + integration tests** (task 6)
`cmd_uninstall.ml`, `main.ml` registration, and `uninstall_expect.ml` must land together — the integration tests invoke the binary, which requires the command to exist.

title: Code review: lib/ simplicity and abstraction
content:
Review of lib/ for simplification opportunities. Motivated by checking whether agent-written code has accumulated unnecessary complexity.

## Accepted suggestions

1. **Repository.Todo / Repository.Note duplication** — Extract a functor parameterized by entity-specific pieces (table name, row parser, status-to-string, default-excluded status, make_id). The two modules become one-liner applications of the functor. Estimated savings: ~150 lines.

2. **Kb_service type re-export bloat** — Instead of copying every sub-service type, re-export them as module aliases or include the sub-service signatures. The bin/ layer can reference e.g. Kb_service.Query.list_spec instead of Kb_service.list_spec. Would cut ~100 lines from .ml and ~90 from .mli.

3. **Note_service and Todo_service are vestigial** — Inline the add logic into Kb_service or Mutation_service and delete the four files (18-19 lines each, but 4 files and unnecessary indirection).

4. **Error-mapping functions scattered and duplicated** — Centralize error mapping. If suggestion 1 is accepted the repository error type itself can be unified, reducing the number of mappers needed. At minimum, Item_service mappings should be the single source.

5. **JSONL todo/note serialization and parsing duplication** — Parameterize _entity_to_json and _parse_entity_record with accessor functions, or leverage the unified entity interface from suggestion 1. ~60 lines of structural duplication.

6. **Data.Item accessor re-wrapping in services** — query_service, relation_service, and delete_service all define local aliases for Data.Item.* accessors. Use Data.Item.* directly at call sites. ~12 lines but reduces mental overhead.

7. **_todo_changed / _note_changed duplication in Mutation_service** — Unify into one _item_changed function by comparing through the Item type. Low priority.

title: Plan: lib/ simplification — reduce duplication and unnecessary abstraction
content:
## Required reading

- docs/index.md
- docs/lib/architecture.md
- docs/lib/principles.md
- docs/lib/correct-construction.md
- docs/product-requirements.md

## Goal

Reduce unnecessary duplication and over-abstraction in lib/ — primarily the near-identical Repository.Todo / Repository.Note modules, the bloated Kb_service type re-export layer, vestigial service wrappers, scattered error-mapping boilerplate, and several smaller duplications. The motivation is to check whether agent-written code has accumulated unnecessary complexity and simplify where warranted.

**Baseline LOC (scripts/loc.sh):**

    bin                  1610
    lib                  6162
    test                 5680
    test-integration     4271
    total                17723

## Current state

### Repository layer — Todo and Note

Repository.Todo (179 lines) and Repository.Note (179 lines) are structural clones. Both define:
- Identical `type t = { db; niceid_repo }` and `type error`
- Identical `exec_sql`, `map_sqlite_error`, `_select_cols`
- Identical `init`, `_insert`, `create`, `import`, `get`, `get_by_niceid`, `update`, `delete`, `list`, `list_all`, `delete_all`
- Differing only in: table name, Data module, default excluded status string

### Data layer — Todo and Note

Data.Todo (65 lines) and Data.Note (67 lines) share identical structure: typeid prefix validation, record type with 7 fields, make, 7 accessors, 4 with_* functions. They differ in status variants and prefix string.

### Service layer — Kb_service

Kb_service.mli is 380 lines — the largest file in the codebase. ~130 lines of its .ml and ~100 of its .mli are verbatim re-declarations of types defined in sub-services (Query_service, Relation_service, Delete_service, Gc_service, Mutation_service, Lifecycle). Every type is copied with `= SubModule.type = { fields... }`.

### Service layer — Note_service and Todo_service

18 and 19 lines respectively. Each wraps a single Repository.create call with error mapping. Used only by Kb_service.add_note and Kb_service.add_todo.

### Error mapping

5+ modules define their own `map_*_repo_error` functions for the same repository error types. Item_service defines the canonical versions, but sync_service, gc_service, delete_service, lifecycle, and kb_service all have their own copies mapping to different service-level error types.

### JSONL serialization

`_todo_to_json` / `_note_to_json` and `_parse_todo_record` / `_parse_note_record` in jsonl.ml are structurally identical (~60 lines of duplication).

### Data.Item accessor wrappers

query_service, relation_service, and delete_service all define local aliases like `let _typeid_of_item = Data.Item.typeid` that add nothing.

### _todo_changed / _note_changed

mutation_service.ml lines 26-34: identical three-field comparison for both entity types.

## Target state

### Data layer

New module `Data.Entity` defines a module type `S` that captures the shared entity interface (constructors, accessors, status handling, entity_name, default_excluded_status). Both `Data.Todo` and `Data.Note` satisfy it with the addition of two values each.

### Repository layer

New module `Repository.Entity_repo` contains a functor `Make(E : Data.Entity.S)` implementing all shared CRUD. `Repository.Todo` becomes `include Entity_repo.Make(Data.Todo)` and `Repository.Note` becomes `include Entity_repo.Make(Data.Note)`. Existing .mli files are preserved — the interface is unchanged.

### Service layer

- Note_service and Todo_service are deleted. Their `add` functions are inlined into Kb_service.
- Error mapping is centralized: Item_service holds the canonical mappers; other services use them via `Result.map_error` composition rather than redeclaring.
- Kb_service exposes sub-services as module aliases (`module Query = Query_service`, etc.) instead of re-declaring all their types. bin/ uses qualified paths.
- _todo_changed / _note_changed unified into one function operating through Data.Item.
- Local Data.Item accessor wrappers removed.

### JSONL

Serialization and parsing parameterized over entity accessors, eliminating the todo/note duplication.

## Impact analysis

### Data layer (lib/data/)
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| entity.ml + entity.mli | **New** — module type S |
| todo.ml + todo.mli | Add entity_name, default_excluded_status |
| note.ml + note.mli | Add entity_name, default_excluded_status |
| item.ml | No change (unchanged) |

### Repository layer (lib/repository/)
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| entity_repo.ml + entity_repo.mli | **New** — functor |
| todo.ml | Rewrite to `include Entity_repo.Make(Data.Todo)` |
| note.ml | Rewrite to `include Entity_repo.Make(Data.Note)` |
| todo.mli | Unchanged (validates functor output) |
| note.mli | Unchanged (validates functor output) |
| root.ml | May need minor adjustment to error matching |

### Service layer (lib/service/)
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| note_service.ml + .mli | **Deleted** |
| todo_service.ml + .mli | **Deleted** |
| item_service.ml + .mli | Stable — canonical error mappers remain |
| mutation_service.ml | Unify _changed; possibly absorb add functions |
| kb_service.ml + .mli | Remove type re-exports, add module aliases, inline add, remove vestigial service handles |
| query_service.ml | Remove local accessor wrappers |
| relation_service.ml | Remove local accessor wrappers |
| delete_service.ml | Remove local accessor wrappers |
| gc_service.ml | Remove duplicate error mappers |
| sync_service.ml | Remove duplicate error mappers |
| lifecycle.ml | Remove duplicate error mapper |
| show_service.ml | No significant change |
| graph_service.ml | No significant change |
| parse.ml | No significant change |

### JSONL (lib/repository/)
| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| jsonl.ml | Parameterize entity serialization/parsing |
| jsonl.mli | No interface change |

### bin/
| Files | Change |
|-------|--------|
| All cmd_*.ml files | Update type references if Kb_service re-exports change |

### test/
| Files | Change |
|-------|--------|
| test/service/note_service_expect.ml | Delete or rewrite |
| test/service/todo_service_expect.ml | Delete or rewrite |
| test/service/test_helpers.ml | Update unwrap helpers if needed |
| Other test files | Update type references as needed |

### test-integration/
| Files | Change |
|-------|--------|
| None expected | Integration tests drive the binary; interface is CLI args |

## Design decisions

### D1: Where does the entity module type live?

**Options:** (a) `lib/data/entity.mli` (b) `lib/repository/entity_repo.mli` inline

**Decision:** (a) `Data.Entity`. The module type only refers to Data-layer types (Typeid, Identifier, Title, Content, Timestamp). It describes "what an entity looks like" which is a Data concern. Placing it in Data also allows JSONL and services to reference it if useful.

### D2: Should the repository functor replace or wrap the existing modules?

**Options:** (a) `include Entity_repo.Make(...)` in existing todo.ml/note.ml (b) remove todo.ml/note.ml and have Root apply the functor directly

**Decision:** (a) Include in existing files. This preserves module paths (`Repository.Todo`, `Repository.Note`), keeps the existing .mli files as the interface contract, and avoids changing Root's type.

### D3: Where do the inlined add functions go?

**Options:** (a) Directly in Kb_service (b) In Mutation_service alongside other mutations

**Decision:** (a) Kb_service. The add functions are trivial one-liners (call Repository.*.create with error mapping). Moving them to Mutation_service would add yet another init dependency. Kb_service already holds the repo handles through Root.

### D4: How to clean up Kb_service re-exports?

**Options:** (a) Module aliases (`module Query = Query_service`) (b) Keep type aliases but without record re-declaration (`type list_spec = Query_service.list_spec`)

**Decision:** (a) Module aliases. This is the most dramatic reduction — all the type blocks disappear from both .ml and .mli. bin/ uses `Service.Query.list_spec` etc. Types that are used everywhere (error, item) stay at the top level.

### D5: JSONL dedup approach

**Options:** (a) Use Data.Entity.S as functor parameter (b) Simple helper function taking accessor arguments

**Decision:** (b) Helper function. A functor is overkill for two JSON serialization functions. A helper like `_entity_to_json ~entity_name ~id ~title ~content ~status ~created_at ~updated_at` is sufficient.

## Atomic groups

### Group A: Entity abstraction + repository functor
Tasks 1 and 2 must land together. The entity module type is defined for the functor; the functor depends on it; and the rewritten repository modules depend on both. Intermediate states don't compile.

### Group B: Kb_service facade cleanup
Task 7 (re-export cleanup) changes the public API surface that bin/ and tests consume. All bin/ and test updates must land with the Kb_service changes.

All other tasks (3–6) can be delivered independently in any order.

title: 9. Create Gc_service and Cmd_gc
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/gc_service.mli` / `gc_service.ml` — **new**
- `lib/service/kb_service.ml` — GC hook in `open_kb`
- `bin/cmd_gc.mli` / `cmd_gc.ml` — **new**
- `bin/cmd_init.ml` — add `--gc-max-age` flag, write value (or 30d default) to config, output confirmation
- `bin/main.ml`
- `bin/dune`
- `test/service/gc_service_expect.ml` — **new**
- `test-perf/perf_scenarios.ml` — `scenario_gc`

## Description
- New `Gc_service` with types: `gc_result = { items_removed; relations_removed }`, `gc_item = { niceid; entity_type; title; age_days }`.
- `val init : Repository.Root.t -> t`
- `val collect : t -> max_age_seconds:int -> now:int -> (gc_item list, Item_service.error) result` — identifies eligible items without removing (dry-run). Groups by connected component via `Graph_service.connected_component`.
- `val run : t -> max_age_seconds:int -> now:int -> (gc_result, Item_service.error) result` — removes eligible items and their relations. Cascade per item: `delete_by_entity` → `delete niceid` → `delete item`.
- GC algorithm: list terminal items → filter by age → compute connected components → remove components where all items are terminal and age-eligible.
- **GC in `open_kb`**: `_run_gc root sync` reads `gc_max_age` from config. If absent, falls back to 30-day default (requirement 21). Calls `Gc_service.run`, and if anything was removed, calls `Sync_service.mark_dirty` + `Sync_service.flush` directly — not via `_with_flush`, since `t` is not yet constructed at this point in `open_kb`.
- **`bs init` change**: `cmd_init.ml` gains `--gc-max-age` optional flag (e.g., `bs init --gc-max-age 14d`). Writes the provided value or 30d default to config. Output includes `GC max age: 30d (default)` or `GC max age: 14d`. JSON output gains `gc_max_age` field.
- `Cmd_gc`: `bs gc` (run), `--dry-run` (show what would be removed), `--set-max-age 14d` (configure), `--show-max-age` (display). ~80 lines.
- Help text: EXAMPLES section covering `bs gc`, `--dry-run`, `--set-max-age`, `--show-max-age`, and `--json` example.
- Perf scenario: 500 items, 1000 relations, half terminal with old timestamps. Gate: < 50ms.

- Perf test: verify < 50ms for 500 items / 1000 relations.

title: Add file_action type and uninstall_file helper
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/lifecycle.ml`
- `lib/service/lifecycle.mli`
- `test/lifecycle_expect.ml`

## Description
Add the `file_action` type (`Deleted | Not_found`) and the `uninstall_file` helper to `lifecycle.ml`/`.mli`.

`uninstall_file` is the simplest uninstall helper — it deletes a file if it exists or reports `Not_found`. Implementation:
```ocaml
let uninstall_file path =
  if Sys.file_exists path then begin Sys.remove path; Deleted end
  else Not_found
```

Export `file_action` and `uninstall_file` in `lifecycle.mli`.

## TDD note
**RED**: Write two unit tests in `lifecycle_expect.ml`:
1. `uninstall_file` on an existing file → returns `Deleted`, file is gone.
2. `uninstall_file` on a non-existent path → returns `Not_found`.

Run tests — they will fail (function does not exist yet or returns wrong output).

**GREEN**: Implement `uninstall_file` and `file_action` type. Run tests — they pass.

title: Add uninstall_agents_md helper
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/lifecycle.ml`
- `lib/service/lifecycle.mli`
- `test/lifecycle_expect.ml`

## Description
Add the `agents_md_uninstall_action` type and `uninstall_agents_md` helper.

Type:
```ocaml
type agents_md_uninstall_action =
  | File_deleted | Section_removed | Section_modified | Not_found
```

`uninstall_agents_md ~directory` implements four cases in order:
1. File does not exist → `Not_found`
2. File contents exactly equal `agents_md_template` → `Sys.remove` → `File_deleted`
3. File contains `"
" ^ agents_md_template` → remove that substring → `Section_removed`
4. File contains `agents_md_section_heading` but neither exact match → `Section_modified`
5. None of the above → `Not_found`

For case 3, use `String.sub` to extract before/after the matched substring and concatenate.

Export in `.mli`.

## TDD note
**RED**: Write tests for all four cases plus a roundtrip (install then uninstall):
1. File does not exist → `Not_found`
2. File was created by init (exact match) → `File_deleted`, file removed
3. File has other content + appended section → `Section_removed`, section stripped, rest intact
4. Section heading present but body edited → `Section_modified`, file unchanged
5. Roundtrip: `install_agents_md` then `uninstall_agents_md` on empty dir → file deleted

Run tests — they fail.

**GREEN**: Implement. Run tests — they pass.

title: Add uninstall_git_exclude helper
content:
## Files
- `lib/service/lifecycle.ml`
- `lib/service/lifecycle.mli`
- `test/lifecycle_expect.ml`

## Description
Add the `git_exclude_uninstall_action` type and `uninstall_git_exclude` helper.

Type:
```ocaml
type git_exclude_uninstall_action = Entry_removed | Entry_not_found
```

`uninstall_git_exclude ~directory` filters `.git/info/exclude` line by line:
1. File does not exist → `Entry_not_found`
2. Split on `
`, filter out lines equal to `db_filename` (i.e., `".kbases.db"`)
3. If no lines removed → `Entry_not_found`
4. Otherwise write filtered content back → `Entry_removed`

Trailing newline preservation: `String.split_on_char '
'` on a file ending with `
` produces a trailing `""`. Since `"" <> db_filename`, it survives filtering and `String.concat "
"` reproduces the trailing newline.

Export in `.mli`.

## TDD note
**RED**: Write tests:
1. Exclude file does not exist → `Entry_not_found`
2. Exclude file contains the entry among other entries → `Entry_removed`, other entries intact, trailing newline preserved
3. Exclude file exists but does not contain the entry → `Entry_not_found`
4. Roundtrip: `install_git_exclude` then `uninstall_git_exclude` → entry removed

Run tests — they fail.

**GREEN**: Implement. Run tests — they pass.

title: Add uninstall_kb orchestrator and re-export through Kb_service
content:
## Files

## Description
Add the `uninstall_result` type and `uninstall_kb` orchestrator to `lifecycle.ml`/`.mli`:

```ocaml
type uninstall_result = {
  directory   : string;
  database    : file_action;
  jsonl       : file_action;
  agents_md   : agents_md_uninstall_action;
  git_exclude : git_exclude_uninstall_action;
}

val uninstall_kb : directory:string option -> (uninstall_result, error) result
```

`uninstall_kb` calls `resolve_directory` then each uninstall helper, assembling the result. It returns `Error` only if directory resolution fails.

Re-export in `kb_service.ml`/`.mli`:
- All four new types (`file_action`, `agents_md_uninstall_action`, `git_exclude_uninstall_action`, `uninstall_result`)
- `uninstall_kb` wrapper that delegates to `Lifecycle.uninstall_kb`

## TDD note
**RED**: Write orchestration-level tests:
1. Full roundtrip: `init_kb` then `uninstall_kb` on a fresh git root → all actions report deleted/removed, files are gone
2. `uninstall_kb` on a git root with no KB → all actions report not found
3. `uninstall_kb` outside a git root → returns `Error`

Run tests — they fail.

**GREEN**: Implement `uninstall_kb` and kb_service re-exports. Run tests — they pass.

title: Centralize error mapping across services
content:
## Files

- lib/service/item_service.ml + .mli (canonical source — already has most mappers)
- lib/service/sync_service.ml (remove _map_todo_error, _map_note_error, _map_relation_error, _map_niceid_error, _map_config_error)
- lib/service/gc_service.ml (remove _map_todo_err, _map_note_err, _map_rel_err, _map_niceid_err)
- lib/service/delete_service.ml (remove _map_todo_err, _map_note_err, _map_rel_err, _map_niceid_err)
- lib/service/kb_service.ml (remove map_note_error, map_todo_error, map_lifecycle_error)
- lib/service/lifecycle.ml (remove map_config_error)

## Description

Item_service already defines the canonical error mappers (map_todo_repo_error, map_note_repo_error, map_relation_repo_error, map_niceid_repo_error, map_config_error). Other services should use these rather than defining their own.

The challenge: sync_service maps to Sync_failed, gc_service to Item_service.error, delete_service to delete_error. The mapping target differs. The approach is:

1. Keep Item_service mappers as the single definition for repo_error -> Item_service.error mapping.
2. Services that need a different target error compose: `Result.map_error (fun e -> MyError (Item_service.map_todo_repo_error e))`.
3. Remove all local re-definitions that duplicate Item_service logic.

For sync_service specifically: its mappers convert to Sync_failed(string). These can be composed: `Result.map_error (fun e -> match Item_service.map_todo_repo_error e with Repository_error msg -> Sync_failed msg | Validation_error msg -> Sync_failed msg)`. Or more simply, extract the string first.

## TDD note

Pure refactor — no behavioral change. All existing tests must pass.
